description:
Pure FieldKind plumbing through `build_frame_schema` and the per-fn schema build site. No FnTypes consultation here — every param slot ships as `FieldKind::FzValue`. Zero behavior change.

## Why this is the atomic shape

The original ticket draft had `build_frame_schema` consult FnTypes here and mark int/float param slots as `RawBytes(8)`. But the GC tracer (kind-aware in heap.rs) SKIPS `RawBytes` slots — and storage at this stage is still tagged FzValue. A typed-float slot holding a Tag::Ptr to a boxed float would silently leak the boxed float on GC. So the schema-kind flip and the storage-format flip must move together — which is exactly what .5.2 and .5.3 do per-kind.

This ticket is the substrate they share: the API surface (`build_frame_schema(name, &[FieldKind])`) without the kind-selection logic.

## Adds

- `build_frame_schema(name: &str, param_kinds: &[FieldKind])` — per-slot kind selection at construction time.
- Per-fn schema-build site passes a `Vec<FieldKind>` of `FzValue` for every param.

## Kills

- The `(name: &str, num_entry_params: usize)` signature of `build_frame_schema`.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test` + fixture matrix green — no behavior change.
- New API ready for .5.2/.5.3 to plug FnTypes-derived kinds into the same call site.

## Out of scope

- FnTypes consultation → .5.2 (float) and .5.3 (int).
- GC tracer correctness when typed slots actually go raw → covered atomically per-kind by .5.2/.5.3.
title: VR.3.1: typed slot kinds (schemas-only infrastructure)
